description: noWhileRead emits error level
noManualArgParsing emits error level
noGroupedLocals emits error level
noExternalBasename emits error level
singleEqualsInTest emits error level
localOrReturn remains error level
is-zsh exits 0 for a .zsh file
steps: Create a temp file named `foo.zsh` with any content

description: is-zsh exits 0 for a no-extension file inside functions/autoload/
steps: Create a temp file at a path containing `functions/autoload/` with no extension, e.g. `/tmp/functions/autoload/my-func`

description: is-zsh exits 0 for a no-extension file with #!/usr/bin/env zsh shebang
steps: Create a temp file with no extension whose first line is `#!/usr/bin/env zsh`

description: is-zsh exits 1 for a no-extension file with #!/usr/bin/env ruby shebang
steps: Create a temp file with no extension whose first line is `#!/usr/bin/env ruby`

description: is-zsh exits 1 for a no-extension file with no shebang
steps: Create a temp file with no extension and no shebang (e.g. just `echo hello`)

description: is-zsh exits 1 for a directory path
steps: Create a temp directory at a path containing `functions/autoload/` with no extension

description: is-zsh exits 1 for a .bats file inside functions/autoload/
steps: Create a temp file at a path containing `functions/autoload/__tests__/` with a `.bats` extension

description: lint-zsh exits 0 with no arguments
lint-zsh exits 0 for only non-ZSH file paths
lint-zsh exits 1 for a ZSH file with violations
lint-zsh exits 0 for a ZSH file with no violations
lintstaged.config.js glob entry is an array with test-bats and lint-zsh
git-file-lint shows formatted violations for a dirty ZSH file
steps: Set up a temp git repo with a `.zsh` file containing a zshlint violation (e.g. `local a b c`)
Mark the file as modified (dirty) in the working tree
Assert output contains a line matching `file:line:col: code: message` format

description: git-file-lint exits silently for a dirty ZSH file with no violations
steps: Set up a temp git repo with a clean `.zsh` file (no violations)

description: git-file-lint exits silently when only non-ZSH files are dirty
steps: Set up a temp git repo with a dirty `.js` file and a dirty Ruby script

description: git-file-lint exits 0 on a clean working tree
steps: Set up a temp git repo with no dirty files

description: git-file-lint skips deleted ZSH files without error
steps: Set up a temp git repo with a `.zsh` file tracked by git
Delete the file so git shows status D
Assert exit code is 0 and no error output
